source: Curated from HKIE Scheme A training requirements, Airport Authority HK Graduate Engineer Programme, VTC Manpower Update Report 2025 (Building & Civil Engineering), HK Labour Department LMI data, JobsDB HK, and Glassdoor HK (2025-2026)

roles.growthOutlook: HK government's Northern Metropolis, Kau Yi Chau artificial islands, and public housing pipeline driving decade-long demand. HKIE Scheme A training programmes at major consultants and contractors. Stable career with professional qualification track.

requiredSkills.description: Strong foundation in structural mechanics, fluid mechanics, geotechnics, and applied mathematics for engineering analysis and design.
Produce engineering drawings and 3D models. BIM (Building Information Modeling) proficiency increasingly mandatory for HK government projects. Revit and Civil 3D are industry standard.
Perform structural analysis and design calculations using industry-standard software. Varies by specialization.
Understanding of Hong Kong Building Ordinance, Building (Construction) Regulations, and relevant Codes of Practice from Buildings Department.
Understanding of construction project lifecycle, site supervision, contract administration, and coordination with contractors and sub-consultants.
Perform engineering calculations, create design spreadsheets, and analyze site monitoring data.
Understanding of HK geology, slope stability, foundation design, and site investigation methods. Critical for HK's hilly terrain.
Prepare design reports, method statements, site investigation reports, and technical submissions to government departments.
Essential for technical documentation, professional exams (HKIE), international design standards, and communication on multinational project teams.
Essential for site communication, contractor coordination, and client meetings. Most construction workers and local clients communicate in Cantonese.
Useful for cross-border infrastructure projects and working with mainland Chinese contractors or consultants.

roles.growthOutlook: HK's position as regional logistics hub and gateway to China ensures steady demand. Growing roles in supply chain analytics, digitalization, and sustainability. E-commerce and cold chain logistics expanding.

requiredSkills.description: Build inventory models, analyze supply chain data, create demand forecasts, and track KPIs. Advanced formulas, pivot tables, and basic macros.
Query supply chain databases, extract inventory and logistics data, and automate reporting workflows.
Create supply chain dashboards showing inventory levels, delivery performance, and cost metrics for management reporting.
Use enterprise resource planning systems for inventory management, procurement, and order processing. SAP is the most common in HK.
Apply statistical methods to forecast demand, plan inventory levels, and optimize stock replenishment cycles.
Understanding of freight forwarding, customs clearance, warehousing operations, incoterms, and HK's trade documentation requirements.
Automate supply chain data analysis, build forecasting models, and create data pipelines for supply chain metrics.
Understanding of green logistics, carbon footprint tracking, and sustainable procurement practices. Growing importance in HK.
Essential for international trade documentation, vendor communication, and working with global supply chain partners.
Required for local warehouse and logistics partner communication, supplier negotiation, and working with HK-based teams.
Essential for supply chain roles involving mainland China — communicating with factories, suppliers, logistics providers, and cross-border trade partners.

roles.growthOutlook: Consistent demand driven by HK's ongoing construction pipeline (public housing, MTR expansion, hospital projects). Specialization in green building, energy efficiency, and smart building systems growing. HKIE Scheme A training available.

requiredSkills.description: Design heating, ventilation, and air conditioning systems for HK's subtropical climate. Understanding of energy efficiency and ACMV design standards.
Design electrical distribution, lighting, fire services, and security systems for commercial and residential buildings.
3D modeling of building services systems using Revit MEP. BIM coordination and clash detection. Mandatory for HK government projects.
Knowledge of HK Buildings Department codes, Fire Services Department requirements, Water Supplies Department regulations, and EMSD standards.
Produce 2D building services drawings, schematics, and coordination plans.
Understanding of green building certification systems. BEAM Plus is the HK standard. Energy modeling and sustainable design principles.
Coordinate building services design with architectural and structural teams. Attend site meetings and resolve installation issues.
Perform engineering calculations for load estimation, pipe sizing, cable sizing, and energy analysis.
Essential for technical specifications, international equipment documentation, and professional examination preparation (HKIE/CIBSE).
Essential for site communication with contractors, coordination with local suppliers, and client meetings.
